title: oehmea sphacelata
intro: Oehmea sphacelata, also known as the Bitter Balsam, is a plant species in the family Rutaceae, native to tropical forests in Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly for its bitter and aromatic compounds, which have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Oehmea sphacelata include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and soothe skin irritations, making it a popular remedy in traditional medicine. Herbal preparations of Oehmea sphacelata can be found in various forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to harness its medicinal properties.
